Web: search the web.
Fetch: fetch a URL.
Agent: delegate a focused task to a sub-agent.
Browser_action: browser/computer-use action. The first token is the action, followed by the action value when needed.
Edit: replace exact existing text in files. Copy oldText exactly from the current file, including indentation. lineRange is only a hint. Use one or more oldText/newText block pairs in the same multiline string.
Write: write a full file.
Todo: set or replace the todo list with markdown checklist items.

Syntax:
read <path> [optional ranges]
Ranges may be inline as path:40-60,80-90,T20, path:L40-60, path:H20, or path:T20.
grep <query> [in <path>]
Use | inside the query for multiple terms.
find <pattern> [in <path>]
Use commas for multiple patterns. Brace globs like *.{ts,tsx} stay as one pattern.
list <path>
Use . as the path value for the current working directory.
web <query>
fetch <url>
agent <prompt>
browser_action <action> [value?]
edit <path>
oldText [lineRange?]:
Exact existing file text
newText:
Replacement text

[Additional oldText/newText blocks in the same string, separated by a blank line]
write <path>
Full file content
todo <optional title>
[ ] item
[-] item
[x] item

Examples:
Examples below are exact command strings.
Do not add quotes, bullets, numbering, backticks, or trailing punctuation.
read src/app.ts:40-60,80-90,T20
grep authservice|pizza|text in src
find package.json,tsconfig.json in src
list .
web python apps
fetch https://example.com
agent analyze the current project structure
browser_action launch https://example.com
edit src/app.ts
oldText 10-12:
const loading = false
newText:
const loading = true

oldText 20-22:
const error = null
newText:
const error = undefined
write notes.txt
build passed
todo Implementation
[ ] Analyze requirements
[-] Update parser
[x] Add tests

calls.description: One complete tool DSL command per string. Must contain at least one command. No prose. Inline commands stay single-line. Edit/write/todo may use multiline bodies, but the header and body must stay in the same string. For edit, every oldText/newText block belongs in that same string.

items.description: A single complete command in the tool DSL. Multiline edit/write/todo commands must include both the header line and the full body in this one string. For edit, include the path header plus all oldText/newText blocks together.

function.name: mcp--github-mcp-server--pull_request_read
function.description: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository.

method.description:
Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 8. get_check_runs - Get check runs for the head commit of a pull request. Check runs are the individual CI/CD jobs and checks that run on the PR.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

function.name: mcp--github-mcp-server--pull_request_review_write
function.description:
Create and/or submit, delete review of a pull request.

Available methods:
- create: Create a new review of a pull request. If "event" parameter is provided, the review is submitted. If "event" is omitted, a pending review is created.
- submit_pending: Submit an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request. The "body" and "event" parameters are used when submitting the review.
- delete_pending: Delete an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request.

method.description: The write operation to perform on pull request review.

function.name: mcp--github-mcp-server--pull_request_read
function.description: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository.

method.description:
Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 8. get_check_runs - Get check runs for the head commit of a pull request. Check runs are the individual CI/CD jobs and checks that run on the PR.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

function.name: mcp--github-mcp-server--pull_request_review_write
function.description:
Create and/or submit, delete review of a pull request.

Available methods:
- create: Create a new review of a pull request. If "event" parameter is provided, the review is submitted. If "event" is omitted, a pending review is created.
- submit_pending: Submit an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request. The "body" and "event" parameters are used when submitting the review.
- delete_pending: Delete an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request.

method.description: The write operation to perform on pull request review.
